entities.name: TRICHODERMA ASPERELLUM
entities.description: A biocontrol fungus used for disease management and genetic enhancement studies.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The agricultural crop targeted for dry root rot disease management.

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting chickpea roots caused by various fungal pathogens.

entities.name: CARBENDAZIM TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a fungus to survive and grow in the presence of the fungicide carbendazim.

entities.name: BIOCONTROL POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of an organism to suppress the population or activity of plant pathogens.

entities.name: N-METHYL-N-NITRO-N-NITROSOGUANIDINE
entities.description: A chemical mutagen used to induce mutations in Trichoderma asperellum.

entities.name: CARBENDAZIM
entities.description: A systemic fungicide used to treat fungal diseases.

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERI
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes wilt and root rot in chickpea.

entities.name: BOTRYTIS CINEREA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen inhibited by Trichoderma asperellum mutants.

entities.name: RHYZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen inhibited by Trichoderma asperellum mutants.

entities.name: TUB2 GENE
entities.description: A gene within the beta-tubulin family associated with carbendazim resistance.

entities.name: BETA-TUBULIN FAMILY
entities.description: A family of proteins involved in microtubule formation, containing the tub2 gene.

entities.name: MYCOPARASITISM
entities.description: The ability of one fungus to parasitize another fungus.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a biocontrol agent to promote the growth of a plant.

entities.name: IN VITRO CONDITION
entities.description: Laboratory-based experimental conditions outside a living organism.

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: A managed setting used to evaluate disease reduction in crops.
